comparison_details.title: Efficacy of ketorolac in the treatment of acute migraine attack: A systematic review and meta-analysis.

items.ai_reason: Title explicitly includes “systematic review and meta-analysis”.
Introduction explains migraine burden and need for review.
Objective to evaluate efficacy of parenteral ketorolac is clearly stated.
Inclusion/exclusion criteria for RCTs, participants, interventions are detailed.
Databases (CENTRAL, Medline, Google Scholar, WHO ICTRP, ClinicalTrials.gov) and search date (Jan 2021) are listed.
Full search strings are not presented.
Two reviewers screened records independently; process described.
Data extraction described but number of independent reviewers not specified.
Primary and secondary outcomes are listed and defined.
Participant characteristics, dosage, etc., are listed as extracted variables.
Risk‑of‑bias assessment method (Cochrane domains) and reviewers are described.
Effect measures (SMD, RR, MD) for each outcome are specified.
Eligibility for each synthesis (comparisons) is explained.
Handling of missing data, contacting authors, and digitising figures are described.
Results are shown in tables, forest plots, and figures.
Random‑effects model, RevMan 5.4, heterogeneity (I²) and rationale are reported.
Only intention to conduct subgroup analyses is mentioned; no methods actually applied.
Sensitivity analysis to assess impact of risk‑of‑bias is described.
Methods for assessing reporting bias (e.g., funnel plots) are only mentioned as intended, not performed.
GRADE methodology for certainty assessment is stated.
PRISMA flow diagram (Figure 1) describing study selection is provided.
Excluded studies and reasons are shown in the flow diagram.
Each included study is cited and its characteristics are tabulated.
Risk‑of‑bias assessments for each study are presented (Figure 2).
For each outcome, study‑level statistics and effect estimates are reported in tables/plots.
Characteristics and risk‑of‑bias of studies contributing to each synthesis are summarised.
All meta‑analytic results, summary estimates, CIs and heterogeneity are presented.
Statistical heterogeneity (I²) and its interpretation are reported.
Sensitivity analysis is mentioned but specific results are not shown.
No assessment of bias due to missing results is presented.
GRADE certainty ratings for each outcome are displayed.
Discussion interprets findings relative to previous reviews.
Limitations of the evidence (small trials, heterogeneity) are discussed.
Review process limitations (language restriction, search completeness) are acknowledged.
Implications for practice and future research are outlined.
PROSPERO registration number is provided.
Protocol is referenced via PROSPERO registration, implying access.
No description of any protocol amendments.
Funding or support for the review itself is not reported.
Competing interests of the authors are not declared.
No statement about public availability of data, code, or materials.
Title explicitly labels the report as a systematic review and meta-analysis.
Background states the objective to evaluate efficacy of parenteral ketorolac for acute migraine.
Inclusion criteria are described, but exclusion criteria are not specified.
Lists databases searched and provides the date up to which they were searched (January 2021).
No method for assessing risk of bias is reported in the abstract.
Abstract reports results but does not describe the synthesis method (e.g., meta‑analysis model).
States 13 trials, 944 participants, and summarizes comparison groups.
Provides effect sizes and p‑values but omits confidence intervals for the main outcomes.
Notes limitation of limited evidence and inadequate number of trials.
Offers interpretation of ketorolac efficacy and implications for future research.
Funding source is not mentioned in the abstract.
No registration details (registry name or number) are provided.
